import pandas as pd
from crabquant.indicator_cache import cached_indicator

DEFAULT_PARAMS = {
    "regime_len": 50,
    "regime_bull": 45,
    "rsi_len": 7,
    "rsi_dip": 42,
    "rsi_exit": 60,
    "ema_len": 50,
}

DESCRIPTION = (
    "Regime-filtered pullback reversion for SPY/QQQ/IWM. "
    "Bullish regime when RSI-50 > 45 and price above EMA-50. "
    "Enters on RSI-7 dip below 42 (no volume filter). "
    "Exits when RSI-7 recovers above 60. "
    "Relaxed entry vs v3 to increase trade frequency."
)


def generate_signals(df: pd.DataFrame, params: dict | None = None) -> tuple[pd.Series, pd.Series]:
    p = {**DEFAULT_PARAMS, **(params or {})}
    close = df["close"]

    rsi_regime = cached_indicator("rsi", close, length=p["regime_len"])
    rsi_fast = cached_indicator("rsi", close, length=p["rsi_len"])
    ema = cached_indicator("ema", close, length=p["ema_len"])

    bullish = (rsi_regime > p["regime_bull"]) & (close > ema)
    dip = rsi_fast < p["rsi_dip"]

    entries = (bullish & dip).fillna(False)
    exits = (rsi_fast > p["rsi_exit"]).fillna(False)

    return entries, exits

previous_attempts.hypothesis: Index ETFs (SPY, QQQ, IWM) exhibit short-term mean reversion after extreme oversold conditions. Combining Bollinger Band lower band penetration with RSI oversold provides high-conviction entry signals, while exiting at the middle band captures the bulk of the reversion move without giving back profits. This should achieve Sharpe 1.5+ with controlled drawdown.

The current strategy is too selective because it requires BOTH price below 2.0std lower BB AND RSI < 30 simultaneously — an extreme conjunction that rarely occurs on index ETFs. By creating three graduated entry pathways (moderate BB+RSI, extreme BB+relaxed RSI, deep RSI standalone), we can 5-10x the trade count while each pathway still captures genuine mean reversion conditions. The 1.5std BB band will catch more pullbacks, the standalone RSI<25 catches panic selloffs, and exits remain conservative at middle band reversion.

Pure mean reversion on SPY/QQQ/IWM fails because these indices trend more than they revert. The 2024 performance (Sharpe 1.14) worked because there were pullbacks within a bull trend, but 2025-2026 degraded as the regime changed. The fix is to add a regime filter: only enter mean reversion dips when the broader trend is bullish (RSI-50 > 50 + price above EMA-50). Using RSI-7 for dip timing generates more signals than waiting for deep RSI-14 oversold, and volume confirmation filters out low-conviction dips. This hybrid approach (mean reversion within trends) should 3-5x trade count while maintaining edge direction.
